qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Unclosed emphasis runs trigger quadratic-time parsing on the default GFM path
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The advisory describes a block-tokenizer infinite-recursion OOM on the `\x09\x0b\n` path through `blockTokens()` and block `text()`, while this finding is a separate inline GFM emphasis rescanning path in `Tokenizer.emStrong()` and `Lexer.inlineText()` on long unclosed `*` runs.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No final qualifying finding identifies the advisory-described `marked` block-tokenizer infinite-recursion root cause that turns the `\x09\x0b\n` input into unbounded memory growth and OOM.
evidence: The advisory ties exploitation to unexpected whitespace handling where `space()` consumes `\x09`, the remaining `\x0b\n` bypasses newline handling, and `blockTokens()` falls through to block `text()` before recursive `inlineTokens()` calls exhaust memory.

The only final qualifying finding in the session is `inline_emphasis_redos`, which cites `src/rules.ts`, `src/Tokenizer.ts:emStrong()`, and `src/Lexer.ts:inlineText()` for a quadratic inline emphasis rescanning path on long unclosed `*` runs.
That finding demonstrates parser CPU slowdown, not the advisory's vulnerable block-tokenizer control flow, `\x09\x0b\n` trigger, or infinite-recursion OOM behavior.
